diagnosis_name: Hyperleukocytosis with / without Tumor Lysis Syndrome

first_line_drugs.notes: Aggressive hydration to maintain urine output and reduce blood viscosity / tumor lysis risk. In asymptomatic hyperleukocytosis: start 2 times maintenance IV fluids. In symptomatic hyperleukocytosis / TLC >2-3 lakhs/cc: start 3-4 times maintenance or approximately 3000 mL/m2/day. Monitor for fluid overload.
For tumor lysis prophylaxis in hyperleukocytosis / high uric acid risk. Dose as per institutional pediatric oncology protocol; start early with hydration.
Use in high-risk tumor lysis syndrome or significant hyperuricemia; preferred when uric acid is elevated or rapid control is needed.
Avoid routine PRBC transfusion before cytoreduction if hyperviscosity is present. Use platelet transfusion for severe thrombocytopenia / bleeding as clinically indicated.

second_line_drugs.notes: Use according to leukemia subtype and oncology protocol for leukoreduction if symptomatic hyperleukocytosis or rapidly rising counts are present.
Urgent leukapheresis or exchange transfusion may be considered in symptomatic hyperleukocytosis with leukostasis or exchange-transfusion-responsive situations.
Consider if tumor lysis syndrome causes refractory hyperkalemia, severe hyperphosphatemia, symptomatic hypocalcemia, fluid overload, or acute kidney injury.

investigations.name: Complete Blood Count with Differential and Platelets
investigations.indication: Diagnosis of hyperleukocytosis and baseline monitoring

investigations.name: Peripheral Smear / Bone Marrow / Flow Cytometry as indicated
investigations.indication: Confirm leukemia subtype and guide cytoreduction

investigations.name: Serum Uric Acid
investigations.indication: Tumor lysis syndrome diagnosis and monitoring

investigations.name: Serum Potassium, Phosphorus, Calcium, Creatinine, Urea
investigations.indication: Cairo-Bishop laboratory TLS assessment and AKI monitoring

investigations.name: Chest X-ray / Neuroimaging as clinically indicated

counselling: This is an oncologic emergency and needs urgent hospital treatment. — यह कैंसर से जुड़ी आपातकालीन स्थिति है और तुरंत अस्पताल में इलाज जरूरी है।

Very high white cell count can block blood flow and affect lungs, brain, kidneys, and heart. — बहुत ज्यादा सफेद रक्त कोशिकाएं खून के बहाव को रोक सकती हैं और फेफड़ों, दिमाग, किडनी और दिल को नुकसान पहुंचा सकती हैं।
Strong hydration and repeated blood tests are required to prevent tumor lysis complications. — ट्यूमर लाइसिस की जटिलताओं से बचने के लिए पर्याप्त पानी और बार-बार खून की जांच जरूरी है।
Breathlessness, drowsiness, seizures, reduced urine output, or chest symptoms must be reported immediately. — सांस फूलना, सुस्ती, दौरे, पेशाब कम होना या सीने में तकलीफ होने पर तुरंत बताएं।
Treatment may include cytoreduction, ICU care, and specialist oncology management. — इलाज में कोशिका कम करने की प्रक्रिया, ICU देखभाल और कैंसर विशेषज्ञ का इलाज शामिल हो सकता है।
warning_signs: Sudden weakness, visual symptoms, or coma
referral_criteria: Immediate referral to pediatric oncologist / intensivist for any hyperleukocytosis with symptoms of leukostasis, TLS, neurological signs, respiratory distress, arrhythmia, or renal dysfunction.
hospitalisation_criteria: All patients with hyperleukocytosis, suspected leukemia with TLC >100000/uL, clinical leukostasis, laboratory TLS, or need for IV hydration / cytoreduction / ICU monitoring require admission.
notes: Hyperleukocytosis is usually defined as WBC/TLC >100000/uL. Leukostasis symptoms may involve pulmonary, CNS, genitourinary, gastrointestinal, and cardiovascular systems. Cairo-Bishop laboratory TLS is present if 2 or more occur within 3 days before or 7 days after therapy: uric acid >=8 mg/dL or 25% rise, potassium >=6 mEq/L or 25% rise, phosphorus >=6.5 mg/dL or 25% rise, calcium <=7 mg/dL or 25% fall. Clinical TLS includes creatinine >=1.5 times upper limit normal, cardiac arrhythmia / sudden death, or seizures.
source: Provided pediatric hyperleukocytosis and tumor lysis syndrome protocol
expected_course: Stabilization depends on early hydration, cytoreduction, and prevention of TLS. Lab abnormalities may worsen over the first few days of treatment and require frequent reassessment.
key_clinical_points: Hyperleukocytosis with leukostasis is an emergency requiring rapid hydration and oncology input.
Tumor lysis syndrome can occur before or after therapy and must be anticipated early.
Avoid unnecessary PRBC transfusion before leukoreduction in severe hyperviscosity unless clearly indicated.

severity_assessment.mild: High TLC without symptoms of leukostasis or TLS; hemodynamically stable.
severity_assessment.moderate: Hyperleukocytosis with rising uric acid / electrolytes or mild respiratory / neurological symptoms.
severity_assessment.severe: Symptomatic leukostasis, TLC often >2-3 lakhs/cc, CNS or pulmonary compromise, arrhythmia, AKI, or clinical tumor lysis syndrome.

monitoring_parameters.parameter: Uric acid, potassium, phosphorus, calcium, creatinine
Fluid balance and signs of overload
